purpose: Time-resolved postprocessor plots + steady-state / oscillation / CLT-uncertainty analysis across all Ethan CFD runs.
scope: All jadyn + modern OpenFOAM runs with postProcessing scalar series (52 dirs; 47 unique after de-duplication). Groups: mdot legs, temperature probes, wall-temperature probes, total_Q net heat.
method_notes: Plots vs time grouped by quantity (mdot / temperature / wall_temperature / heat); last-300s companion with OLS trendline, equation + R^2 in legend.
RMSE about mean (= RMS fluctuation) and RMSE about trend (detrended oscillation), plus variance and peak-to-peak, quantify oscillation.
Steady-state verdict: relative-to-mean drift when the mean is well separated from zero, else scale-free drift_ratio (drift / oscillation amplitude) to avoid the near-zero-mean trap.

Uncertainty of the mean via CLT: naive SEM = sigma/sqrt(N) and autocorrelation-corrected SEM = sigma/sqrt(N_eff), N_eff = N / tau_int; SEM-vs-averaging-time convergence plotted against a 1/sqrt(t) reference.
Ran locally in ~2.5 min (scalar data ~200 MB); no overnight job needed (threshold was 30 min).
Pure-Python stats + stdlib-only SVG so it rebuilds in a minimal HPC shell.
key_findings: mdot and temperature/wall-temperature are steady in almost all runs; loops reach a stationary (gently oscillating) state.
total_Q is the NET heat residual (~0.1 W vs hundreds-of-W loop scale); its 'not steady' verdicts reflect a small residual's relative noisiness, not thermal runaway.

The only mdot rows flagged not-steady are the salt3 corrected-Q perturbations (hi5q/hi10q) whose mass flow is still moving — independently consistent with the corrected-Salt gate 'too short / needs extended continuation'.
open_questions_for_user: Window is 300 s of simulated time; rerun with --window-seconds to change it.
Optional: aggregate per-patch wallHeatFlux.dat for an absolute (non-residual) heat-vs-time series if a thermal steadiness verdict on absolute heat is wanted.
